content:
# How Much is My Scrap Car Worth? Get an Honest UK Price Guide

Many factors determine **how much your scrap car is worth** in the UK. These include its weight, current scrap metal prices, the presence of valuable parts (like a catalytic converter), and its overall completeness. Your car's location can also play a role, though BuyAndScrap.com offers free nationwide collection. To get an accurate, instant price for your vehicle, the best way is to use a trusted online valuation tool.

## What Influences Your Scrap Car's Value in the UK?

It’s not just a guess! Many things affect the price you’ll get for your end-of-life vehicle. Understanding these helps you know what to expect.

### Vehicle Weight

For most genuine scrap cars, the main factor is its weight. Authorised Treatment Facilities (ATFs) pay by the tonne for the metal content. Heavier vehicles, like large saloons or SUVs, usually fetch more than smaller hatchbacks. This is simply because there's more metal to recycle.

### Current Scrap Metal Prices

Scrap metal prices change regularly on the global market. What your scrap car is worth today might be different next month. Reputable buyers, like us at BuyAndScrap.com, track these prices daily. This ensures you always receive a competitive and up-to-date offer.

### Make, Model & Age: Salvageable Parts

Even a 'scrap' car might have valuable parts. If your vehicle is no longer roadworthy but has working components, its value can increase. Engines, gearboxes, or specific body panels from certain makes and models can be worth more. These parts can be reconditioned or reused, adding a premium to the scrap metal value.

### Your Car's Location

Where your car is located can impact its value. If it's in a remote area, collection costs for a scrap dealer might be higher. This could slightly reduce your offer. Thankfully, BuyAndScrap.com provides free nationwide collection. So, your postcode won't affect the price we offer.

### Completeness & Condition

Has your car been stripped of parts? Is it missing its engine, wheels, or catalytic converter? A largely complete car will typically get a better price. The catalytic converter is particularly important. It contains precious metals like platinum, palladium, and rhodium. Its presence can significantly boost your car's scrap value. Always declare if it’s missing when getting a quote.

## Ready to Find Out How Much Your Scrap Car is Worth?

## Common Questions About Scrapping Your Car

### Do I need an MOT to scrap my car?

No, you do not need a valid MOT certificate to scrap your car. Scrap vehicles are usually collected and taken directly to an Authorised Treatment Facility (ATF). They are not driven on public roads. So, an MOT isn't required for this process. If your car is still roadworthy and you're unsure if it's truly scrap, you can always check its MOT status with our [Free MOT Checker](/mot-checker).

### What paperwork do I need to scrap my car in the UK?

You will typically need your V5C registration document (logbook). The scrap dealer will complete the relevant section (section 4 for 'permanent export or to be scrapped') and send it to the DVLA. You should keep the small yellow section (V5C/3) as proof. If you don't have your V5C, you can still scrap your car, but it's vital to inform the DVLA. You might also need photo ID.

### What happens to my old car after it's scrapped?

Once collected, your car goes to an Authorised Treatment Facility (ATF). Here, it’s de-polluted safely, meaning all hazardous fluids and materials are removed. Then, it's dismantled and crushed. Most of the materials, especially metal, are recycled according to strict environmental guidelines. This ensures your old car causes minimal environmental impact.
